hero.description: I build inclusive, performant web applications with the React ecosystem. Passionate about ensuring the web is usable by everyone, regardless of ability.

about.label: About
about.paragraph1: I'm a frontend developer with a deep commitment to digital accessibility. With multiple certifications in web accessibility standards (WCAG, ARIA, RGAA), I help organizations create products that are not just beautiful, but truly usable by everyone.
about.paragraph2: My expertise lives at the intersection of the React ecosystem and Node.js, where I build scalable, performant applications with accessibility baked in from the ground up — not bolted on as an afterthought.

portfolio.title: Portfolio
portfolio.subtitle: Selected projects showcasing my work in frontend development and digital accessibility.

tennisLab.title: Tennis Lab
tennisLab.description: A full-stack application for managing real-time tennis match data, player stats with a focus on accessibility and performance.

accessibilityDashboard.title: Accessibility Audit Dashboard
accessibilityDashboard.description: Internal tool for tracking and reporting accessibility compliance across multiple web properties with automated testing integration.

principal.description: Leading accessibility initiatives across product teams. Implementing WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, conducting audits, and mentoring developers on inclusive design patterns within a React/TypeScript stack.

frontend.description: Built and maintained large-scale React applications with a focus on performance and accessibility. Integrated automated a11y testing into CI/CD pipelines.

web.description: Developed responsive web applications for big companies. (McDonald's, PSG, Orange, etc.) Gained experience in modern JavaScript frameworks and best practices, while collaborating closely with designers and backend teams.

consultant.description: Started professional career as a consultant at Orange, working on QA. This role provided a strong foundation in web technologies and client collaboration.

items.dequeDesigningUx: Deque University - Designing an Accessible User Experience
items.access42: Access 42 - Développer des sites web accessibles

tennis.title: Tennis
tennis.description: Player, Official, and board member of my tennis club. The sport that keeps me sharp on and off the court.

cooking.title: Cooking
cooking.description: Experimenting with flavors and techniques. Cooking is my creative outlet outside of code.

chess.title: Chess and Go
chess.description: Strategizing and challenging my mind. Chess and Go are games of patience and foresight.

guitar.title: Guitar
guitar.description: Strumming chords and learning new songs. Music is how I unwind and recharge.

blog.label: Blog
blog.title: Thoughts on Accessibility & Frontend Development
blog.subtitle: Sharing insights, tutorials, and best practices to help developers build more inclusive web experiences.
